title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Delight
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi with our whole slow roasted recipe. Tender lamb, aromatic rice, and spices for an unforgettable dinner.
longTailKeywords: how to make Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
h2Headings: Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Lamb Ouzi
Tips for the Best Slow Roasted Lamb
Frequently Asked Questions About Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi dish, garnished with nuts and herbs, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of the Middle East with this spectacular whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, perfect for special occasions.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a magnificent Middle Eastern dish featuring tender, slow-roasted lamb served atop aromatic spiced rice. This recipe brings the authentic flavors of the region to your table, perfect for celebrations and family gatherings. The meat becomes incredibly succulent, falling off the bone and infusing the rice with its rich essence.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb shoulder or leg dry. In a small bowl, mix 1 tablespoon of seven spice, 1/2 teaspoon of cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Rub this spice mixture generously all over the lamb. Make a few deep incisions into the lamb with a sharp knife to allow the spices to penetrate deeper.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until deeply browned, about 5-7 minutes per side. This step builds flavor and creates a beautiful crust. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.

instructions.title: Aromatics and Braising Liquid
instructions.text: In the same pot, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Add the chopped onions and cook until softened and translucent, about 8-10 minutes. Add the minced garlic, bay leaves, and cardamom pods, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Deglaze the pot with a splash of broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Return the seared lamb to the pot. Pour in enough hot chicken or lamb broth to come halfway up the sides of the lamb. Bring to a simmer, then cover the pot tightly with a lid or foil. Transfer to a preheated oven at 160C (325F) and slow roast for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and falling off the bone. Flip the lamb halfway through cooking.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spiced Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb roasts, prepare the rice. Drain the soaked basmati rice. In a separate large pot, heat a tablespoon of olive oil. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of seven spice and 1/2 teaspoon of cinnamon, stirring for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the drained rice and toast it lightly for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add 3 cups of hot broth (or the cooking liquid from the lamb if desired, strained) and salt to taste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Once the lamb is cooked, carefully remove it from the pot and let it rest for 10 minutes. Shred the lamb meat off the bone using two forks. Fluff the spiced rice. To serve, spread a generous layer of spiced rice on a large platter. Arrange the tender shredded lamb over the rice. Garnish generously with toasted almonds, pine nuts, and fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately with yogurt or a fresh cucumber salad on the side.

tips: For the most tender lamb, ensure the pot is sealed tightly during slow roasting to retain moisture. You can also add a little more broth if it starts to dry out.

The seven spice blend (Baharat) is crucial for authentic flavor. If unavailable, you can create a similar blend with equal parts allspice, black pepper, cinnamon, coriander, cumin, cloves, and nutmeg.
Leftover Lamb Ouzi can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently in the microwave or oven, adding a splash of broth to keep the rice moist.
article:
Unveiling the majestic centerpiece of Middle Eastern feasting, Lamb Ouzi is more than just a meal; it's a celebration, a testament to culinary heritage, and a symphony of flavors that captivates every palate. Imagine a succulent whole lamb, slow-roasted to perfection, its tender meat falling off the bone, infused with aromatic spices, and often served dramatically over a bed of fragrant, spiced rice, studded with nuts and dried fruits. This isn't your everyday dinner; it's a dish reserved for grand occasions, family gatherings, and moments that call for something truly extraordinary. If you're ready to embark on a culinary adventure that will impress and delight, then mastering the art of the Lamb Ouzi Recipe is your next delicious challenge.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, also spelled as Ouzy or Ghoozi, is a traditional Middle Eastern dish renowned for its grandeur and rich flavors. Originating from various parts of the Levant and Gulf regions, it typically involves a whole lamb or a large cut, like a leg or shoulder, slow-cooked until incredibly tender. What sets Ouzi apart is not just the roasted lamb, but the intricate stuffing or accompanying rice. This rice is often a masterpiece in itself, cooked with a blend of spices like cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, and nutmeg, and enriched with minced meat, toasted nuts (almonds, pistachios, pine nuts), and sometimes dried fruits like raisins. The result is a harmonious blend of savory, sweet, and aromatic notes, creating a truly unforgettable dining experience that embodies the warmth and generosity of Middle Eastern hospitality.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Lamb Ouzi requires attention to detail and quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need for this impressive Lamb Ouzi Recipe:

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb (typically 15-25 lbs/7-11 kg), cleaned and trimmed (ask your butcher)
*   Alternatively, 1 large lamb leg or shoulder (5-7 lbs/2.3-3.2 kg)
*   Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

For the Marinade/Rub:
*   1/2 cup olive oil
*   1/4 cup lemon juice
*   1 head garlic, cloves minced
*   2 tablespoons ground cumin
*   2 tablespoons ground coriander
*   1 tablespoon smoked paprika
*   1 tablespoon dried oregano
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   Pinch of ground cloves (optional)
*   Fresh rosemary sprigs (optional, for cavity)

For the Spiced Rice Stuffing (or accompanying rice):
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed
*   1 lb (450g) ground lamb or beef
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   4 cups chicken or beef broth
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   1 tablespoon ground cumin
*   1 tablespoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   Salt and pepper to taste
*   1/2 cup toasted slivered almonds
*   1/2 cup toasted pine nuts
*   1/4 cup raisins or dried cranberries (optional)
*   Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Lamb Ouzi

Preparing a whole slow-roasted lamb might seem daunting, but this Lamb Ouzi Recipe breaks it down into manageable steps, ensuring a show-stopping result.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the whole lamb (or large cut) dry with paper towels. Score the fatty parts of the lamb with a sharp knife in a crosshatch pattern, being careful not to cut into the meat too deeply. This helps the marinade penetrate and fat render. Season generously inside and out with salt and pepper.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, oregano, cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves (if using).
3.  **Marinate the Lamb:** Rub the marinade all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scored areas. For a whole lamb, you can also place some fresh rosemary sprigs inside the cavity. Cover the lamb and refrigerate for at least 6-8 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to meld.
4.  **Pre-Roast Preparation:** Remove the lamb from the refrigerator 1-2 hours before roasting to bring it closer to room temperature. Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Place the lamb on a large roasting rack set inside a sturdy roasting pan. Add 1-2 cups of water or broth to the bottom of the pan to keep the lamb moist and prevent drippings from burning.
5.  **Slow Roasting:** Roast the lamb, uncovered, for 4-6 hours for a large leg/shoulder, or 8-12 hours for a whole lamb, depending on its size. The internal temperature should reach 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare, 160°F (71°C) for medium, and 170°F (77°C) for well-done. For the last hour, you can increase the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) to crisp up the skin. Baste occasionally with pan juices.
6.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for at least 30-45 minutes (longer for a whole lamb) before carving. This is crucial for tender, juicy meat.
7.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice:** While the lamb rests, prepare the rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ground lamb/beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat. Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat. Pour in the broth, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
8.  **Assemble and Serve:** Gently fluff the rice with a fork. Stir in the toasted almonds, pine nuts, and raisins (if using). For a whole lamb, you can either stuff the cavity with the rice or serve the rice alongside the carved lamb. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley.

## Tips for the Best Slow Roasted Lamb

Achieving that melt-in-your-mouth tenderness and rich flavor for your Ouzi is all about technique.
*   **Don't Rush the Marinade:** The longer the lamb marinates, the deeper the flavors will penetrate. Overnight is truly best.
*   **Low and Slow is Key:** Roasting at a lower temperature for a longer period breaks down tough connective tissues, resulting in incredibly tender meat.
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable:** This allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, preventing it from drying out when carved. Don't skip this step!
*   **Baste for Moisture and Flavor:** Regularly basting the lamb with its own pan juices keeps it moist and builds a beautiful, flavorful crust.
*   **Monitor Internal Temperature:** A meat thermometer is your best friend. It takes the guesswork out of roasting and ensures perfect doneness.
*   **Adjust Spices to Taste:** Feel free to tweak the spice blend in both the marinade and the rice to suit your preference. A little more cardamom or a touch of allspice can make a difference.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a feast in itself, but it shines even brighter when accompanied by complementary side dishes.
*   **Fresh Salads:** A crisp Fattoush or Tabbouleh salad provides a refreshing contrast to the richness of the lamb and rice.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like a basic Tzatziki without the dill) or plain labneh (strained yogurt) offers a cooling counterpoint.
*   **Roasted Vegetables:** Alongside the lamb, serve a medley of roasted vegetables such as carrots, potatoes, bell peppers, or zucchini, seasoned with olive oil and herbs.
*   **Warm Pita Bread:** Essential for scooping up any delicious juices or leftover rice.
*   **Pickles and Olives:** A selection of Middle Eastern pickles and olives adds a tangy bite that cuts through the richness.

## Variations and Substitutions for Ouzi

While the classic Lamb Ouzi Recipe is magnificent, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or available ingredients.
*   **Lamb Cut:** If a whole lamb is too ambitious, a large leg of lamb, shoulder, or even a bone-in lamb roast works wonderfully. Adjust cooking times accordingly.
*   **Meat for Rice:** Instead of ground lamb or beef, you can use finely diced chicken or even omit meat entirely for a vegetarian rice option.
*   **Grains:** While basmati rice is traditional, you could experiment with other long-grain rice varieties or even a mix of rice and freekeh (roasted green wheat) for a different texture and nutty flavor.
*   **Spice Blend:** Experiment with different Middle Eastern spice blends. Baharat is a common all-purpose spice mix that would work beautifully. A touch of saffron threads steeped in warm water can also be added to the rice for color and aroma.
*   **Nuts and Fruits:** Customize the nuts and dried fruits based on what you have. Cashews, walnuts, dried apricots, or dates can all add delightful textures and flavors.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi (Inspired):** While not traditional, you could create a vegetarian version using a whole roasted cauliflower or large butternut squash as the centerpiece, stuffed with the spiced rice.

## Frequently Asked Questions About Lamb Ouzi

**Q: How long does it take to prepare Lamb Ouzi?**
A: The active preparation time is about 1-1.5 hours, but the lamb requires significant marinating time (6-8 hours or overnight) and slow roasting time (4-12 hours depending on the cut). Plan for at least 24 hours from start to finish for the best results, though much of that is passive cooking.

**Q: Can I make Lamb Ouzi ahead of time?**
A: You can marinate the lamb a day or two in advance. The spiced rice can also be prepared a few hours ahead and gently reheated. For the best flavor and texture, the lamb is best served fresh from its resting period. Leftovers, however, are delicious and can be stored in the refrigerator for 3-4 days.

**Q: Is this dish difficult to make for a home cook?**
A: While it looks impressive, the Lamb Ouzi Recipe is more about patience than complex techniques. The steps are straightforward, but the long cooking times require planning. With a good meat thermometer and a large enough oven, it's very achievable for an enthusiastic home cook.

**Q: What if I don't have a roasting rack big enough for a whole lamb?**
A: You can improvise by creating a "rack" with large carrots and onions cut into thick pieces, or by crushing aluminum foil into thick ropes and arranging them at the bottom of the pan to elevate the lamb.

Lamb Ouzi is truly a culinary masterpiece, capable of transforming any gathering into an unforgettable feast. The aroma alone will draw everyone to the table, and the taste will transport them to the heart of Middle Eastern hospitality. Don't be intimidated by its grand appearance; with this detailed Lamb Ouzi Recipe, you're well-equipped to create a dish that will be talked about for years to come. Enjoy the process, savor the flavors, and delight in the joy of sharing this magnificent meal.
